---
title: "Olfactory Commodification and the 'By Mennen' Jingle as Miniature Kulturindustrie"
subtitle: "If You Heard the Jingle in Your Head, You're Old and Really Should Monitor Your Calcium Intake"
format: 
    qarticle3-pdf:
      keep-tex: TRUE
thanks: "Replication files are available on the author's Github account [http://github.com/svmiller/stevetemplates](http://github.com/svmiller/stevetemplates). **Current version**: `r format(Sys.time(), '%B %e, %Y')`; **Corresponding author**: steve@svmiller.com. A user who is familiar with my templates may observe some redundancy with the title footnote and information you can include in the author or paper info fields. That point is well-taken. Do with this what you want."
abstract: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nam eget consectetur diam. Pellentesque id eros auctor, eleifend sem a, sagittis nisi. Nam vel nisi eget metus consectetur hendrerit nec ut justo. Ut metus eros, ullamcorper in condimentum non, eleifend lacinia mi. Cras sodales, arcu eu fringilla efficitur, lorem purus maximus mauris, id volutpat dui lectus non velit. Aliquam scelerisque nulla rutrum facilisis laoreet. Cras tristique et lorem ac feugiat. Duis eu purus eu ante tristique suscipit. Etiam aliquet egestas tortor et scelerisque. Nullam vulputate quam sed diam dictum, vitae pellentesque nisl fermentum. Curabitur ac rutrum lacus. Proin pellentesque, elit et malesuada porttitor, neque velit pretium dui, sit amet fermentum turpis turpis sed felis. Curabitur urna neque, bibendum non fringilla et, accumsan at orci. In blandit mauris urna, ac accumsan urna vehicula a. Phasellus iaculis nisi id nibh euismod malesuada venenatis in elit. Aenean diam mi, dapibus nec egestas sit amet."
keywords: "pandoc, r markdown, knitr, god help me why am I doing this"
n-author: TRUE
author:
- name: Forename diMcPatronymixsonovez
  number: 1
  email: steven.miller@ekohist.su.se
  affiliations:
    - ref: su
- name: Previous Employer
  email: previous@clemson.edu
  affiliations:
    - ref: clemson
- name: Visiting Lecturer
  email: visitor@illinois.edu
  affiliations:
    - ref: illinois
- name: A Graduate Student
  email: gstudent@ua.edu
  affiliations:
    - ref: bama
- name: Same Employer
  email: same.employer@ekohist.su.se
  affiliations:
    - ref: su
- name: An Undergraduate Student
  email: undergrad.1234@osu.edu
  corresponding: TRUE
  affiliations:
    - ref: osu
affiliations:
  - id: su
    name: Stockholm University
    department: Institutionen för ekonomisk historia och internationella relationer
    address: Universitetsvägen 10A, 114 18 Stockholm, Sweden
  - id: clemson
    name: Clemson University
    department: Department of Political Science
    address: 232 Brackett Hall
    city: Clemson
    region: SC
    postal-code: 29634
    country: USA
  - id: illinois
    name: University of Illinois
    department: Department of Political Science
    address: 1407 W Gregory Dr, Urbana, IL 61801, USA
  - id: bama
    name: University of Alabama
    department: Department of Political Science
    address: Box 870213, Tuscaloosa, AL 35487, USA
  - id: osu
    name: The Ohio State University
    department: Department of Political Science
    address: 411 Woody Hayes Dr, Columbus, OH 43210, USA
geometry: margin=2.5cm
bibliography: inst/refs.bib
biblio-style: apsr
#cite-method: natbib
#csl: chicago-notes-bibliography-16th-edition.csl
# citeproc: TRUE
mainfont: cochineal
anonymous: FALSE
sansitup: FALSE
# sansfont: Libertinus Sans
fontsize: 12pt
# doublespacing: TRUE
endnotes: FALSE
pandocparas: TRUE
alternate-layout: TRUE
remove-emails: TRUE
remove-paper-info: FALSE

paper-info:
  - "*Last updated*: `r format(Sys.time(), '%e %B %Y')`"
  - "*Word Count*: 10,000 (pinky swear)"
  - "*Github*: [svmiller/stevetemplates](http://github.com/svmiller/stevetemplates)"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(kableExtra)
library(stevedata)
library(tidyverse)
# Uncomment below if you want code captions
# oldSource <- knitr::knit_hooks$get("source")
# knitr::knit_hooks$set(source = function(x, options) {
#   x <- oldSource(x, options)
#   x <- ifelse(!is.null(options$code.cap), paste0(x, "\\captionof{chunk}{", options$code.cap,"}"), x)
#   ifelse(!is.null(options$ref), paste0(x, "\\label{", options$ref,"}"), x)
# })
# Add `chunkcaption: TRUE` to YAML as well.
```



# Introduction

\kant[1]

## A Subsection in the Introduction

\kant[2]

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.


When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# Results

\kant[24]

## Including Plots

I've published a few things in the past, a lot with my adviser. These include @millergibler2011dtnc, @gibleretal2012iia, @giblermiller2012cfap, @giblermiller2013qv, @giblermiller2014etts, and  @gibleretal2016amid (among others). Cite my solo stuff too. These citations include @miller2013tdpi, @miller2017ieea, @miller2017etst, and @miller2018etttc. These are just a few to get started.[^more]

[^more]: I have more things of mine you can cite.

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot. This is Quarto, so you can reference it through something like @fig-mtcars.


\kant[25]

```{r}
#| fig-cap: A Plot of mtcars
#| label: fig-mtcars
plot(mtcars)
```

\kant[8-10]

@tbl-mitchell68 is a data set from an analysis in 1968 that I talk about on my blog.[^mitch68]

[^mitch68]: https://svmiller.com/blog/2024/10/inequality-insurgency-south-vietnam-1968-statistical-analysis/

```{r, echo= F}
#| tbl-cap: Mitchell's (1968) Data Set
#| label: tbl-mitchell68
#| 
Mitchell68 %>% select(-id) %>%
  kbl(., col.names = c("Province", "Govt. Control",
                       "Owner-Operated Land", "CV Land-Holdings",
                       "Vietnamese Land", "French Land", "Mobility",
                       "Pop. Density"),
      align = c("lccccccc"),
      linesep = "",
      booktabs = TRUE, longtable = TRUE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 7)

```

# References
